Crstl.note: EDI/order management, mentioned in ops contexts. Episode #162 (Ops Nightmares) features Teva Vogelstein from Crstl discussing chargebacks.

Vividly.note: Trade promotion management. Low mention count suggests low brand awareness among CPG founders.

QuickBooks.note: Default accounting tool for early-stage CPG brands. Mentioned in inventory and ops contexts.

Northall.note: CPG finance consultancy. One dedicated episode with Ryan Williams.

Graphite.note: Financial planning for CPG. More mentions than Settle.

Ampla.note: Not mentioned at all despite being a known CPG finance tool.

SPINS.note: Most-mentioned data tool. Deeply embedded in CPG vocabulary — buyers reference SPINS data constantly.

IRI.note: Lower awareness than SPINS among emerging brands.

Crisp.note: Retail analytics. Low but growing mentions.

SPS Commerce.note: EDI platform. One dedicated episode (#159). Well-known among ops-focused brands.

DrivePoint.note: Forecasting tool. One dedicated episode (#218) generated all mentions.

Cin7.note: Not mentioned directly (though episode #139 features Cin7's Cory Hebert).

easyops_competitive_landscape.key_gap: NO deduction-specific management tool is mentioned ANYWHERE in 361 episodes. Crstl is the closest (EDI/order management with chargeback implications), but it's positioned as an operations tool, not a deduction recovery tool. Vividly (trade spend) has only 3 mentions. The deduction management category essentially doesn't exist in this ecosystem's vocabulary.
